gene_description: Proprotein Convertase Subtilisin/Kexin Type 1 — endoprotease that converts prohormones (proinsulin, POMC) to active forms. Critical for appetite regulation and energy balance. Variants increase obesity risk through impaired prohormone processing.

source.title: Contribution of common non-synonymous variants in PCSK1 to body mass index variation and risk of obesity: a systematic review and meta-analysis with evidence from up to 331,175 individuals
source.finding: PCSK1 rs6232 (N221D) and rs6235 (S690T) minor alleles are associated with 1.34-fold and 1.22-fold increased obesity risk respectively. Effects consistent across Caucasian, African, and Hispanic ethnicities. Variants impair prohormone processing including proinsulin and POMC.

AA.effect: Homozygous N221D carriers have significantly impaired proprotein convertase 1/3 activity. Elevated proinsulin levels indicating impaired proinsulin-to-insulin conversion. Increased BMI and strong obesity risk. May present with features of mild proprotein convertase deficiency.
AA.recommendation: Intensive weight management and metabolic monitoring. Proinsulin levels should be monitored. GLP-1 agonists may help with appetite regulation. High-protein, low-glycemic diet to manage insulin resistance. Consider referral to endocrinologist.

GA.effect: Heterozygous N221D carriers show mildly elevated proinsulin/insulin ratio and increased BMI. Moderate obesity risk increase. Partially impaired prohormone processing.
GA.recommendation: Weight management strategies. Regular metabolic screening. Balanced caloric intake with emphasis on satiety-promoting foods.

AG.effect: Heterozygous N221D carriers show mildly elevated proinsulin/insulin ratio and increased BMI. Moderate obesity risk increase. Partially impaired prohormone processing.
AG.recommendation: Weight management strategies. Regular metabolic screening. Balanced caloric intake with emphasis on satiety-promoting foods.

GG.phenotype: Normal PCSK1 activity at this locus
GG.effect: Wild-type N221 with full proprotein convertase activity. Normal proinsulin processing.

Homozygous S690T carriers show reduced PCSK1 biosynthesis and activity. Elevated proinsulin levels. Increased BMI and 1.22-fold obesity risk per allele. Effect consistent across Caucasian, African, and Hispanic populations but not seen in East Asians.
GG.recommendation: Weight management focus. Monitor proinsulin/insulin ratio. High-fiber, high-protein diet to enhance satiety. Regular physical activity.

CG.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate PCSK1 activity and modest BMI increase.
CG.recommendation: Standard healthy diet with emphasis on portion control. Regular exercise.

GC.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate PCSK1 activity and modest BMI increase.
GC.recommendation: Standard healthy diet with emphasis on portion control. Regular exercise.

CC.phenotype: Normal PCSK1 activity at this locus
CC.effect: Wild-type S690 with full proprotein convertase activity.
